source_notes: Windows Package Manager and winget configuration: Microsoft Learn
WSL installation and distro management: Microsoft Learn
.NET SDK and WPF target framework support: Microsoft Learn
Android Studio, Android SDK, platform-tools, and emulator detection: Android Developers
Tool package IDs validated against winget data where practical; source notes are informational, not claims of popularity.

tools.description: Microsoft's package manager for Windows apps and developer tools.
tools.why_it_matters: It is the safest default installer and updater for this dashboard.

tools.description: Modern cross-platform PowerShell runtime.
tools.why_it_matters: It gives scripts a current shell with better module and automation support.

tools.description: Modern terminal host for PowerShell, WSL, Git, and developer shells.
tools.why_it_matters: It keeps Windows, Linux, and cloud shells usable in one place.

tools.description: Distributed version control system.
tools.why_it_matters: Git is required for source control, open-source workflows, and most scaffolding tools.

tools.description: Command-line interface for GitHub repositories, pull requests, auth, and workflows.
tools.why_it_matters: It enables secure GitHub auth, PR workflows, issue work, and Codespaces-style automation without storing tokens here.

tools.description: Large file support for Git repositories.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for assets, binaries, models, game tools, and repositories with large files.

tools.description: Lightweight editor for most languages and GitHub workflows.
tools.why_it_matters: It is the most broadly useful editor for scripts, apps, APIs, WSL, and AI-assisted coding.

tools.description: Microsoft build toolchain for native and .NET projects.
tools.why_it_matters: Needed for C++ build dependencies, Windows desktop app builds, NuGet packages with native code, and some Python packaging.

tools.description: SDK and CLI for building .NET apps, APIs, desktop apps, and tools.
tools.why_it_matters: Required for C#/.NET apps, WPF/WinUI projects, APIs, tests, and many Windows tools.

tools.description: Built-in .NET formatter and analyzer runner.
tools.why_it_matters: Keeps C# projects formatted and catches analyzer issues.

tools.description: Package manager CLI for .NET packages.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for legacy package workflows and inspecting package feeds.

tools.description: Python runtime for automation, scripts, data tools, and packaging.
tools.why_it_matters: Python is essential for automation, tooling, tests, packaging, and Codex-assisted workflows.

tools.description: Windows Python launcher used as the `py` command.
tools.why_it_matters: It makes multiple Python versions easier to run and is expected by many Windows Python workflows.

tools.description: Python package installer.
tools.why_it_matters: Needed for package installs inside virtual environments.

tools.description: Installs Python CLI tools into isolated environments.
tools.why_it_matters: It avoids polluting global Python packages while making tools available on PATH.

tools.description: Fast Python package, project, and environment manager.
tools.why_it_matters: It speeds up Python dependency management and is now a high-value default for new Python work.

tools.description: JavaScript runtime and npm package manager.
tools.why_it_matters: Required for web apps, TypeScript, frontend tools, and many code-generation workflows.

tools.description: Default Node.js package manager.
tools.why_it_matters: Needed for installing JavaScript and TypeScript dependencies.

tools.description: Fast, disk-efficient JavaScript package manager.
tools.why_it_matters: A strong default for modern TypeScript projects and monorepos.

tools.description: Java Development Kit for compiling and running Java apps.
tools.why_it_matters: Required for Java apps, Android tooling, Gradle, and many cross-platform tools.

tools.description: Java compiler included with a JDK.
tools.why_it_matters: Confirms a full JDK is installed, not only a runtime.

tools.description: Java build and dependency management tool.
tools.why_it_matters: Common for Java libraries, apps, and enterprise projects.

tools.description: Build system used for Java, Kotlin, and Android projects.
tools.why_it_matters: Required by many Android and Java/Kotlin workflows.

tools.description: File archiver for installers, dependencies, packaged builds, and archives.
tools.why_it_matters: Used for archives, installers, packaged builds, ROM/tools workflows, and extracting dependencies.

tools.description: Fast text search for codebases.
tools.why_it_matters: Essential for fast code navigation and AI-assisted coding workflows.

tools.description: Fast file finder for developer workflows.
tools.why_it_matters: Makes project navigation and script automation faster.

tools.description: JSON command-line processor.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for APIs, automation, GitHub output, and config inspection.

tools.description: YAML command-line processor.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for GitHub Actions, Kubernetes, Docker Compose, and YAML configs.

tools.description: Official Android IDE and SDK manager.
tools.why_it_matters: Required for full Android app development and managing SDK components.

tools.description: SDK folder used by Android build tools, platform tools, and emulator packages.
tools.why_it_matters: Android builds need a valid SDK path even when the IDE is installed.

tools.description: ADB command-line tool for devices and emulators.
tools.why_it_matters: Needed to deploy, debug, and inspect Android apps.

tools.description: Windows Subsystem for Linux for Linux command-line development.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for Linux-targeted tools, shells, Docker workflows, and cross-platform validation.

tools.description: Desktop container runtime for Windows.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for APIs, services, databases, Linux containers, and reproducible local stacks.

tools.description: Runs code checks before commits.
tools.why_it_matters: It catches formatting, lint, and policy mistakes before they reach GitHub.

tools.description: Fast Python linter and formatter.
tools.why_it_matters: It catches Python mistakes quickly and can replace several older lint/format tools.

tools.description: Python test runner.
tools.why_it_matters: It is the default practical test tool for Python automation and packages.

tools.description: Static type checker for Python.
tools.why_it_matters: It catches type and interface mistakes before runtime.

tools.description: Python type checker used by Pylance.
tools.why_it_matters: It gives fast Python type diagnostics in editor and CI workflows.

tools.description: Packages Python scripts as Windows executables.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for distributing small automation tools to machines without Python setup.

tools.description: Python virtual environment helper.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful when projects need virtualenv behavior beyond built-in venv.

tools.description: Python packaging and dependency manager.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for projects already standardized on Poetry.

tools.description: Python compiler for optimized standalone builds.
tools.why_it_matters: Optional for advanced Python packaging and performance-oriented builds.

tools.description: Typed JavaScript compiler.
tools.why_it_matters: Core tool for modern web apps, APIs, and frontend tooling.

tools.description: JavaScript and TypeScript linter.
tools.why_it_matters: Catches common code errors and consistency issues in JS/TS projects.

tools.description: Code formatter for JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, Markdown, and more.
tools.why_it_matters: Keeps frontend and config files consistent with minimal fuss.

tools.description: Fast JavaScript and TypeScript test runner.
tools.why_it_matters: A high-value test runner for modern Vite and TypeScript projects.

tools.description: Alternative JavaScript package manager.
tools.why_it_matters: Optional for projects that already use Yarn.

tools.description: Fast source code counter.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for understanding project size and language makeup.

tools.description: Static checker for GitHub Actions workflows.
tools.why_it_matters: Catches GitHub Actions YAML mistakes before CI fails.

tools.description: Static analyzer for shell scripts.
tools.why_it_matters: Useful for Bash scripts in WSL, CI, and cross-platform projects.

tools.description: Static analysis and security scanning tool.
tools.why_it_matters: Optional deeper scanning for larger or security-sensitive repositories.
